purpose: Tracks the competitor-driven enhancement loop (every 30m). One tool per iteration, rotating. Tax/paycheck calculator is OUT OF SCOPE.

history.shipped: Adjustable quiet-zone (margin) selector — Standard (4 modules) / Narrow (2) / None (0). Replaced the hardcoded QUIET=4 constant in qr.js with a clamped quietZone() reader (0-8, default 4) driven by a new #qrMargin <select> in the template; both render() (canvas) and svgString() (SVG export) now use the live value so preview, PNG, and SVG all honor the chosen margin. The selector lives inside #qrForm so the existing input listener re-renders on change with no new wiring. Tighter/zero margins let the code embed flush in dense layouts; Standard stays the scan-safe default. Closes the quiet-zone/margin backlog idea (QRCode Monkey exposes a 'Set margin'/quiet-zone control we lacked). No new deps; dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Autosave to localStorage so a refresh doesn't lose work. render() and downloadPdf() now serialize the full form (business/client fields, invoice meta, currency, dates, tax/discount, brand color, notes, all line items, and the optional logo dataURL) to localStorage key 'tb.invoice.v1'; init() restores it via applyState() before first render (restoring flag suppresses re-save during repopulation, dates are only seeded on a truly fresh visit so a cleared date isn't re-filled). Added a 'Clear & start fresh' button (confirm-gated) that wipes storage and resets to the shipped defaults, plus an autosave reassurance line in the template. Also hardened itemRow to attribute-escape desc/qty/rate (new attr() helper) since restored values flow back through the same value="..." interpolation. Competitor parity: InvoiceSimple/Invoice-Generator/Zoho all persist a draft so reloads don't wipe input; we lost everything on refresh. No new deps; dist gitignored, rebuilt at deploy.

Selectable print-sheet size (4×6 in / 5×7 in / A4). Added a 'sizes' array to photo-specs.json printSheet (standard physical paper dims @300DPI: 4x6=1200×1800, 5x7=1500×2100, A4=2480×3508 — NOT country biometric specs, so no data-sourcing conflict), a sheetSize <select> in the template, and selector wiring in photo-maker.js (sheetSpec tracks the choice; downloadSheet tiles onto the chosen sheet; filename + status use the size id/label; falls back to the legacy single 4×6 when no sizes array). The generic cols/rows tiling already handled arbitrary sheet dimensions. Closes the paper-size-selector gap every competitor (PixConvert, MakePassportPhoto, Cutout.Pro, Passport Photo Snap) offers — we hardcoded 4×6. No new deps; PNG/JPG format selector unchanged.

Optional padding/margin around the circle (toggle + amount slider). Added CanvasEditor.setPadding, which shrinks the shape (and its border ring, image clip, and bg fill) inward by a fraction of the half-size, leaving a transparent margin so the avatar isn't flush to the edge. Implemented in the shared _drawTo so preview and exported PNG match at any output size; pad cascades through outerR -> inset -> innerR. FrameToolkit explicitly offers 'add padding' around the circle; this closes that gap. No new deps; source only (dist is gitignored, rebuilt at deploy).

SEO/copy fix: the page already supports 7 payload types (URL, WiFi, vCard, Email, SMS, Phone, Text) but the <title>, meta description, og:title/og:description, and JSON-LD description still only advertised 'WiFi, vCard & URL'. Updated all four to enumerate the full set, recovering long-tail search coverage (email/sms/phone/text QR) competitors rank for. No JS/logic change; content-only.

Brand color picker. A color input (defaulting to the site accent #1a7f37, with a Reset button, hex-validated) recolors the INVOICE heading and the grand-total separator rule in both the live preview and the exported PDF (jsPDF setTextColor/setDrawColor). Closes the brand-color gap competitors like SmoothLedger and InvoiceSimple offer; no new deps.

Percentage discount option. The discount field now has a type selector (amount | %); a percent discount is applied to the subtotal pre-tax, and the percent is labelled in both the live preview and the PDF totals. Closes the amount-only gap competitors don't have.

Optional logo upload (PNG/JPG) — client-side FileReader, shown in live preview and embedded in the PDF header via jsPDF addImage. Competitor parity (logo/branding was the most common feature we lacked).

Foreground/background color pickers (HTML color inputs). Applied to the canvas render, the preview holder background, and the SVG export. Color customization was the universal feature every competitor had and we lacked.

Four new payload types: Email (mailto: with subject/body), SMS (SMSTO:number:message), Phone (tel:), and Plain text. Added options to the type selector, dedicated input groups in the template, and encoders in buildPayload(); also widened the input listener to cover textareas so the new multiline fields trigger live re-render. Email/SMS/phone/text are universal across competitors (QR Dex, the-qrcode-generator) and were our biggest payload-type gap.

Optional circular border/ring (toggle + color picker + thickness slider). Implemented in the shared CanvasEditor (_drawTo): thickness is a fraction of the radius so preview and exported PNG match at any output size; the image is clipped inside the ring. Avatar-style borders were a common competitor feature (Watermarkly et al.) we lacked.

Compliance-style head-position guide. Replaced the single averaged oval with the actual allowed head-height band: a crown line, a shaded chin band spanning the spec's headMin–headMax mm, crown/chin labels, plus a post-upload instruction stating the required head-height range. No new deps; SVG-only (no face detection, no fabricated specs). Competitors (PhotoGov, Pic4Pass, PersoFoto) all sell head-position compliance feedback we lacked; AI background removal was deliberately skipped as too heavy for a micro-commit.

Background fill option (toggle + color picker). When enabled, the transparent area behind the crop is filled with a solid color; for the circle shape only the inner disc is filled so the corners stay transparent (PNG alpha preserved). Implemented in the shared CanvasEditor _drawTo so preview and exported PNG match at any output size. Fotor offers PNG-transparent vs solid-fill output; this closes that gap and is the prerequisite for a future JPG export.

JPG output option alongside PNG. Added a download-format selector (PNG lossless / JPG); the choice is wired through both the single-photo and 4x6-sheet exports via CanvasEditor.toBlob type+quality. JPG is opaque because the rect editor already fills the frame with the spec background (white/light-grey), so no alpha-flattening fix was needed. Closes the JPG gap every competitor (PixConvert, PhotoGov) offers — print labs/kiosks prefer JPG. No new deps, no spec-data changes.

invoice-generator: Shipping cost line (Flowlu) — separate from tax/discount in totals
Extend brand color to the table-header underline + BILL TO/DATE labels for fuller theming
qr-code-generator: Center logo overlay (QRCode Monkey) — needs forced High ECC + center clearing
Low-contrast warning when fg/bg are too close (scannability guard)
crop-image-into-circle: JPG output option alongside PNG (Fotor) — needs a forced background fill since JPG has no alpha (background fill now shipped, so this is unblocked)
Rotate / straighten the image before cropping (Watermarkly)
Batch mode — crop multiple images to circles at once (Watermarkly, imagy)
passport-photo-maker: Background readiness hint — sample corner pixels and warn if the background is dark/busy vs the spec's required white/grey (no AI, just pixel stats)
AI/WASM background removal to white — heavy (in-browser model); the high-value but expensive parity feature (PhotoGov, Passport Photo Snap)
Print-ready PDF export of the sheet (PixConvert offers JPG + PDF; we have JPG/PNG only) — jsPDF is already a dep on other tools
More country specs (40+ on PixConvert) — only add with authoritative government sources; escalate conflicts per data rule
Grid-line / cut-guide preview overlay before downloading the sheet (cut guides already drawn into the export, but not previewed)
